summary: Bangladesh faced multiple infrastructure and safety challenges today as reports emerged of a Tk 40 million overbridge already in disrepair after just two months and 438 road deaths in June, with motorcyclists accounting for a third of victims. The government tightened regulations on official imagery while the National Board of Revenue warned of tax audit fraud schemes and introduced new minimum tax requirements for first-time filers. A severe heatwave swept 22 districts as the country grappled with education concerns, including missing HSC exam candidates and a ragging incident that led to student suspensions. On the economic front, smartphone prices faced downward pressure, internet usage continued climbing, and the agriculture sector reported record aman rice output of 1.73 crore tonnes.
